rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use assign only in implementation annexes for output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation annexes
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
generic_rules: Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Use range comparisons only for numeric scalar data types
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit exposure
